items.headline: Guy Carbonneau Montreal Canadiens lights torch Game 4
items.description:
Habs fans, the captain is back.
Guy Carbonneau — who captained the 1993 Montreal Canadiens Stanley Cup championship team — lit the torch at Bell Centre on Tuesday to kick off the festivities before Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Buffalo Sabres.
Carbonneau fired up the

items.headline: Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen to start in goal for Buffalo in Game 4 of Eastern 2nd Round
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen started for the Buffalo Sabres in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Montreal Canadiens at Bell Centre on Tuesday.
The goalie had backed up Alex Lyon the past seven games after being pulled 16 seconds into the third period of a 4-2

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes taking advantage of break leading up to Eastern Conference Final
items.description: RALEIGH, N.C. -- The Carolina Hurricanes returned to practice on Tuesday, three days after a four-game sweep of their Eastern Conference Second Round series against the Philadelphia Flyers. No one reaped the benefits of the short series more than coach Rod Brind’Amour.

items.headline: Charlie McAvoy suspended six regular-season games for actions in Boston Bruins game
items.description:
NEW YORK -- Boston Bruins defenseman Charlie McAvoy has been suspended for six regular-season games, without pay, for slashing Buffalo Sabres forward Zach Benson during Game 6 of the teams’ First Round series in Boston on Friday, May 1, the National Hockey League’s Department of Player Safety announced today.

items.headline: Minnesota Wild need 'absolute best' to keep season alive in Game 5
items.description: MINNEAPOLIS -- The Minnesota Wild are angry. They are frustrated. They hated how they played Monday night at home, hated that the result, a 5-2 loss to the Colorado Avalanche in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round, was appropriate and earned for how they competed against what was

items.headline: Colorado must keep playing with 'urgency' in Game 5 vs Minnesota
items.description:
DENVER -- The Colorado Avalanche returned home feeling optimistic about their chances of closing out the Minnesota Wild in Game 5 of the Western Conference Second Round at Ball Arena on Wednesday (HBO MAX, truTV, TNT, SN, TVAS, CBC).
Colorado holds a 3-1 lead in the best-of-7 series and are

items.headline: Joel Eriksson Ek Jonas Brodin wont travel with Minnesota Wild Game 5 Western 2nd Round
items.description: MINNEAPOLIS -- The Minnesota Wild will still be without Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin for Game 5 of the Western Conference Second Round against the Colorado Avalanche at Ball Arena in Denver on Wednesday (8 p.m. ET; HBO MAX, truTV, TNT, SN, TVAS, CBC).

items.headline: Pittsburgh Penguins GM Kyle Dubas would love to re-sign forward Evgeni Malkin
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- The Pittsburgh Penguins "would love" to re-sign Evgeni Malkin, general manager Kyle Dubas said Tuesday.
Malkin could become an unrestricted free agent on July 1 after completing the final season of a four-year, $24.4 million contract ($6.1 million average annual value). The three-time Stanley Cup champion forward

items.description:

Montreal leads best-of-7 series 2-1

items.headline: Josh Manson fined maximum for butt-ending Michael McCarron
items.description: NEW YORK – Colorado Avalanche defenseman Josh Manson has been fined $5,000, the maximum allowable under the Collective Bargaining Agreement, for butt-ending Minnesota Wild forward Michael McCarron during Game 4 of the teams' Second Round series in Minnesota on Monday, May 11, the National Hockey League's Department of Player Safety

items.headline: Owen Tippett internal bleeding missed second round of playoffs for Philadelphia
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Owen Tippett did not play for the Philadelphia Flyers in the Eastern Conference Second Round because of an internal bleeding issue, the forward said Tuesday.
Tippett sustained the injury during the Flyers' six-game series win against the Pittsburgh Penguins in the first round.

items.headline: Mats Zuccarello still enjoying game with Wild on brink in West second round
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Mats Zuccarello still feels the tingles going through his body when he walks out of the dressing room, down the tunnel and steps onto the ice before every game.

items.headline: NHL EDGE stats Mitch Marner breakout Stanley Cup Playoffs 2026
items.description: NHL.com's fantasy staff continues to cover the latest trends and storylines in the League through the lens of NHL EDGE puck and player tracker stats. Today, we identify some key advanced metrics behind Vegas Golden Knights forward Mitch Marner.
